Buy Now Pay Later for Singapore Airlines Flights

Yes, Laters lets you book Singapore Airlines flights and split the cost with Atome, Kredivo, or PayNow, with interest-free options available"—basically mentioning the popular payment providers directly to be more concise for the LLM, rather than just saying "leading BNPL providers.

Atome

Pay in 3 interest-free instalments

Available in Singapore, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Thailand, and other Southeast Asian markets.

Atome is one of Southeast Asia's most popular BNPL services, and it pairs naturally with Singapore Airlines bookings given its strong presence in SQ's home markets. Split your Singapore Airlines fare into 3 equal interest-free instalments — the first at checkout, the remaining two automatically charged over the following weeks. Whether you're booking a quick hop to Bangkok or a long-haul flight to London, Atome makes spreading the cost simple and transparent. No hidden fees, no interest — just three clean payments.

Paynow

Instant bank transfer via PayNow

Available in Singapore.

PayNow is Singapore's real-time bank transfer network, and it is about as local as it gets for SQ's home market. Singapore-based travellers can pay for their Singapore Airlines booking on FlyFairly directly from their bank account in seconds, with no card fees and no third-party middlemen. It is the no-fuss option for those who prefer to pay in full instantly and move on to the exciting part: packing. Supported by all major Singapore banks, PayNow is reliable, secure, and perfectly suited for booking flights with Singapore's own national carrier.

Kredivo

3, 6, or 12 monthly instalments

Available in Indonesia and Thailand.

Kredivo is Indonesia's leading digital credit platform, offering flexible instalment terms of 3, 6, or 12 months for Singapore Airlines bookings. For Indonesian travellers flying SQ routes from Jakarta or Bali through Singapore, Kredivo provides a familiar and trusted credit option. Longer instalment terms make it particularly practical for premium cabin bookings or peak-season travel where fares are higher. Subject to Kredivo's standard credit approval process.

Our take on Singapore Airlines

Reliability & operations

Singapore Airlines delivers one of the most consistent operational records in global aviation. With an on-time rate of 87% and a cancellation rate of just 1.2%, it outperforms most long-haul competitors on reliability — and its zero safety incidents over the past decade reinforces a level of trust that few airlines can match. The fleet averages 7.2 years old, meaning passengers are almost always flying modern equipment. Changi Airport as the primary hub also helps: consistently ranked the world's best airport, it reduces the ground-side friction that often derails connections on other carriers.

Comfort & in-flight experience

Economy seats offer 32 inches of pitch and 17 inches of width — standard for a full-service carrier but not exceptional. Where Singapore Airlines pulls ahead is the in-flight experience: the KrisWorld entertainment system scores 4.8 out of 5 across passenger reviews, making it one of the highest-rated IFE systems in the industry. Business class passengers get full flat beds and, on certain aircraft, private suites with sliding doors. Meals are consistently rated at 4.2 out of 5, with the Book the Cook programme allowing business class passengers to pre-select from an extended menu — a feature passengers frequently cite as a differentiator. Wifi is available across the fleet but consistently flagged as slow and expensive relative to the ticket price, which is the most common complaint in passenger reviews.

Value & network

At $0.07 per km, Singapore Airlines sits in the mid-to-premium range — cheaper than Emirates on comparable long-haul routes but more expensive than budget carriers. The value-for-money Skytrax score of 3.9 reflects this honestly: passengers broadly feel they get what they pay for, but the premium is felt. The airline serves 76 destinations across a network built around long-haul routes, with Star Alliance membership giving access to lounges and codeshares across 26 partner airlines. For travellers primarily flying within Southeast Asia on shorter routes, the premium may be harder to justify; for long-haul passengers, particularly in business class, it consistently ranks among the top three carriers globally.

Popular Singapore Airlines Routes

Singapore Airlines connects Changi to some of the most-travelled routes in Asia and beyond. Here are the routes travellers book most — compare fares and find your best deal on FlyFairly.

SIN → CGK

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇮🇩 Jakarta Soekarno-Hatta. One of SQ's busiest regional routes, connecting two of Southeast Asia's great capitals with multiple daily flights and seamless connections onward.

CGK → SIN

🇮🇩 Jakarta Soekarno-Hatta → 🇸🇬 Singapore Changi. The return leg is equally popular — whether you're heading home, transiting, or beginning a longer journey through Changi's world-class terminals.

HKG → SIN

🇭🇰 Hong Kong International → 🇸🇬 Singapore Changi. A premium business corridor linking two of Asia's most dynamic financial hubs, with strong demand year-round from both leisure and corporate travellers.

BKK → SIN

🇹🇭 Bangkok Suvarnabhumi → 🇸🇬 Singapore Changi. A perennially popular route for tourists, expats, and business travellers alike — Bangkok and Singapore are arguably Southeast Asia's two most visited cities.

SIN → BKK

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇹🇭 Bangkok Suvarnabhumi. Flying SQ to Bangkok means you arrive at Suvarnabhumi on one of the most reliable carriers on the route, with strong on-time performance and quality service.

SIN → HKG

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇭🇰 Hong Kong International. Whether it's dim sum, business, or a connecting long-haul flight, SQ's Singapore–Hong Kong service is a staple of the Asian aviation landscape.

SIN → MNL

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇵🇭 Manila Ninoy Aquino. A high-frequency route serving one of the largest overseas Filipino communities in the world, as well as business travellers and leisure seekers.

DPS → SIN

🇮🇩 Bali Ngurah Rai → 🇸🇬 Singapore Changi. Post-holiday blues hit differently when you're flying Singapore Airlines home from Bali — at least the service is good enough to soften the blow.

SIN → DPS

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇮🇩 Bali Ngurah Rai. Start your Bali escape the right way — Singapore Airlines' Changi-to-Denpasar service offers a polished, comfortable ride to the Island of the Gods.

SIN → TPE

🇸🇬 Singapore Changi → 🇹🇼 Taipei Taoyuan. Taipei has become one of Asia's most exciting travel destinations, and SQ's direct service from Singapore makes it an effortless city-break choice.

Top Destinations with Singapore Airlines

From the gleaming terminals of Changi to the rice terraces of Bali, Singapore Airlines serves some of the world's most compelling destinations. Here's where travellers are flying.

Singapore

The airline's home — and for good reason. Singapore is a city-state that somehow manages to be futuristic, historic, and delicious all at once, and Changi Airport makes every arrival feel like an event.

Bali (Denpasar)

Bali remains one of the world's most beloved island destinations, and Singapore Airlines' Denpasar service makes it one of the more civilised ways to arrive. Temples, terraces, and terrific food await.

Bangkok

Bangkok is chaotic, glorious, and utterly addictive — a city of golden temples, street food at midnight, and rooftop bars with views that make you forget you have an early flight. Singapore Airlines flies here frequently.

Hong Kong

Few cities pack as much energy into so little space as Hong Kong. Singapore Airlines connects Changi to Hong Kong multiple times daily, making it one of the easiest premium short-haul options in Asia.

Jakarta

Indonesia's sprawling capital is a major business and cultural hub, and Singapore Airlines operates one of the most reliable connections between Changi and Soekarno-Hatta International Airport.

Manila

Manila is a city of contradictions — frenetic and warm, overwhelming and welcoming. SQ's Singapore–Manila service is a popular choice for both Filipino travellers and those discovering the Philippines for the first time.

Shanghai

Shanghai Pudong is one of Singapore Airlines' key East Asian gateways, connecting China's most cosmopolitan city to the wider SQ network with reliable, high-quality service.

Taipei

Taipei has quietly become one of Asia's most exciting cities — brilliant night markets, world-class museums, and a food culture that demands a return visit. Singapore Airlines flies direct from Changi.

Singapore Airlines: What Every Traveller Should Know

Singapore Airlines Cabin Classes — Which Is Right for You?

Singapore Airlines offers four cabin classes: Suites, Business Class, Premium Economy, and Economy. Suites — available on select A380 routes — are widely regarded as among the finest private air travel experiences in the world. Business Class features full flat-bed seats on long-haul routes, while Premium Economy offers a meaningfully upgraded experience over standard Economy.

Singapore Airlines Baggage Allowance — What's Included per Class?

Checked baggage allowances generally vary by route, fare type, and cabin class. Economy passengers typically receive 25–30 kg, while Business and Suites passengers generally enjoy higher allowances. Carry-on allowance is typically 7 kg in Economy. Always verify your specific allowance on your booking confirmation or Singapore Airlines' official website before travel.

Singapore Airlines Check-In — How to Avoid Airport Fees

Online check-in opens 48 hours before departure and is free of charge. Please note: Singapore Airlines charges a fee for airport check-in at select airports. Checking in online via the Singapore Airlines app or website — or managing your booking at singaporeair.com/managebooking — is strongly recommended to avoid this fee.

How Does KrisFlyer Work? Miles, Tiers & Redemption Explained

Singapore Airlines' frequent flyer programme, KrisFlyer, allows members to earn miles on SQ flights and partner airlines. Miles can be redeemed for flights, upgrades, and more. Elite tiers include KrisFlyer Elite Silver and Elite Gold, with the top-tier PPS Club for frequent premium travellers. Singapore Airlines is a member of the Star Alliance, enabling mile earning and redemption across a vast global network.

Split Your Extras Too — Not Just the Fare

Add ancillaries at checkout alongside your fare and split the entire total — extras included — across your chosen BNPL instalments.

Seat selection

Window, exit row, or bulkhead — pick your spot before someone else does.

Extra baggage

Add checked luggage allowance without the airport-counter panic.

Meal preferences

Pre-select your in-flight meal, including special dietary options.

Sports & oversized baggage

Surfboards, golf clubs, and bicycles — added and paid in instalments.

Cancellation protection

Optimism is lovely. A safety net is better.

Lost baggage cover

Covered if your bag goes somewhere you didn't.

Build my booking

BNPL for Flights — What You Need to Know

Common questions about splitting your flight cost with Buy Now, Pay Later.

Why use BNPL for flights?

Buy Now, Pay Later lets you lock in today's fare and spread the cost over weeks or months. Instead of paying the full price upfront, you make smaller, predictable payments — ideal when you book ahead or are managing a tight budget.

Is BNPL really free?

Many BNPL plans on FlyFairly are interest-free when you pay on time. Some providers charge interest or late fees if payments are missed. Always review the provider's terms before confirming your instalment plan.

Does BNPL affect my credit score?

Most BNPL providers use a soft credit check, which does not affect your score. However, missed payments can be reported to credit agencies depending on the provider. Paying on time keeps your credit healthy.

Late payments & cancellations

Missing an instalment may result in late fees and could affect your credit rating. If your flight is cancelled, any refund is applied to your outstanding BNPL balance first. Contact FlyFairly support for guidance on your specific booking.
